title: KLA Corp. Will Benefit From CHIPS Act And The Infrastructure Supercycle
text: The semiconductor manufacturing equipment market is expected to expand from $87b in 2021 to $210b by 2031. In accordance with the CHIPS act, over $50b will be allocated over the next five years for the development of semiconductor manufacturing in the U.S.

title: KLA Corporation: Strength In A Troubled Semi-Market
text: Just when you think it's safe to “get back in the water,” Intel (NASDAQ: INTC) comes out with an earnings report that has every semiconductor name from Micron (NASDAQ: MU) to KLA Corporation (NASDAQ: KLAC) moving lower. The takeaway from the Intel report is that weakness in all segments, not just the core segment, was compounded by inventory bloat that points to troubled times for the entire semiconductor industry.

title: KLA (KLAC) Q2 Earnings and Revenues Top Estimates
text: KLA (KLAC) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of 4.24% and 5.47%, respectively, for the quarter ended December 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: KLA forecasts Q3 revenue below estimates
text: U.S. chip toolmaker KLA Corp on Thursday forecast third-quarter revenue below Wall Street estimates, sending its shares down about 5.3% after market.

title: KLA Corp. stock sinks as outlook disappoints following drop in chip demand
text: Shares of KLA Corp. slid in after-hours trading Thursday after the semiconductor-equipment maker forecast third-quarter profit and sales that came in below expectations, amid fading chip demand.

title: KLA Corporation Reports Fiscal 2023 Second Quarter Results
text: Total revenues were $2.98 billion, above the guidance range of $2.65 to $2.95 billion; GAAP diluted EPS attributable to KLA was $6.89 and non-GAAP diluted EPS attributable to KLA was $7.38, each finishing within the respective guidance ranges; Cash flow from operating activities and free cash flow were $688.3 million and $594.6 million, respectively; and Capital returns were $539.2 million, split between $184.2 million in dividends paid and $355.0 million in share repurchases. MILPITAS, Calif.

title: KLA Corp.: My Top Equipment Company With Strong Tailwinds From National Chip Incentive Programs
text: The U.S. CHIPS Act is being mirrored by eight Chip Incentive Programs at other countries. The majority of new fabs planned are foundry and logic fabs.

title: KLA Corporation: Executing Well In A Tough Environment
text: KLA Corporation has been executing well despite a tough macro environment for the semiconductor industry. The company has been growing operating earnings and has a robust capital return plan in place.

title: Memory And Logic: Two Different Chips, Two Different Trajectories In 2023
text: Micron Technology, Inc. and other memory companies have substantial inventories to work through, as revenues plummet. The logic/foundry IC sector will not exhibit the revenue plunge in 2023 as will the memory IC sector.

title: Semiconductors Winners And Losers At The Start Of 2023
text: The year 2022 was bad for semis even though business was mostly good, which provides an important clue as to how 2023 could play out. Fundamentals in the semiconductor industry deteriorated as 2022 went by, but stocks did the opposite by getting better heading into 2023.
